content.meaning:
1. predictions, offers, promises, requests, suggestions
+ e.g. Will you help me with the dishes? / Shall I get you some coffee?
2. on-the-spot decisions
+ e.g. "Your clothes are dirty." "Are they? I'll wash them."
3. when it is not certain whether sth will happen (prediction)
+ e.g. If they come, I'll buy a bottle of wine.
4. opinions, hopes, fears esp. with think, expect, suppose, etc.
+ e.g. I think he'll pass his test.

1. intention
+ e.g. She's going to take her driving test in a month.
2. planned actions
+ e.g. I know my clothes are dirty. I'm going to wash them tomorrow.
3. when there is evidence that sth will definitely happen
+ e.g. She's going to have a baby.
4. action seen as a result of sth else in the future
+ e.g. He doesn't know how to light a fire. He's going to burn himself.

1. action in progress at a certain time in the future
+ e.g. I'll be flying to Paris this time tomorrow.
2. logical assumption about the present
+ e.g. He will be sleeping now. (It's midnight.)
3. already planned action (instead of Present Cont.)
+ e.g. I'll be seeing Sam tonight. Would you like me to tell him the news?

1. action finished before a certain future time
+ e.g. By the end of July he will have been in Athens for two months.
2. logical assumption in the past
+ e.g. It's mid-night. She will have gone to sleep by now.

1. duration of an action up to a certain time in the future
+ e.g. By next Sunday, they will have been painting that house for two weeks.

by/before are used with Future Perfect in positive sentences; until is used in negative sentences.
+ e.g. She will have cleaned the house by 6 o'clock.
+ e.g. She won't have cleaned the house until 6 o'clock.

(future plans)
+ e.g. The meeting is to take place on Wednesday.

content.word: be about + infinitive / be on the point of + gerund
content.meaning:
(immediate future)
+ e.g. They are about to leave.
+ e.g. They are on the point of leaving.
